title: Molecular Distillation Apparatus
description: The user operates a steampunk-inspired chemical distillation apparatus. The coffee mug serves as a reaction vessel, the gears represent a mechanical stirring and heating mechanism, and the corset acts as a molecular sieve filter. Users must adjust temperature, stirring speed, filter tightness, reagent concentration, and extraction timing to successfully distill a glowing compound.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Load coffeeMug.glb as the central reaction vessel positioned at origin, gears.glb positioned below-left as the mechanical heating and stirring drive, and Corset.glb positioned to the right as a molecular sieve filter connected by a glowing tube (use TubeGeometry along a CatmullRomCurve3). Set up a PerspectiveCamera at (0,3,6) looking at origin, with ambient light intensity 0.4 and two directional lights for dramatic lab lighting. Add a dark background color (0x1a1a2e). Create an HTML overlay panel with these controls: (1) a Temperature slider 0-500°C that changes the mug's emissive color from blue to orange to red, (2) a Stirring Speed slider 0-100 RPM that rotates the gears model proportionally around its Y-axis each frame, (3) a Filter Tightness slider 1-10 that scales the corset model on its X-axis between 0.7 and 1.3 simulating compression, (4) a Reagent Concentration dropdown with options 0.1M/0.5M/1.0M/2.0M that changes a particle system inside the mug (use Points with varying count), (5) a Start Extraction button that triggers a 5-second animation of glowing particles (small spheres) traveling along the tube from mug to corset, and (6) a Purge button that resets all parameters. Validate extraction success: temperature must be 200-350°C, stirring 40-70 RPM, filter tightness 4-7, and concentration 1.0M. If valid, the corset glows green and a success message appears. If invalid, it glows red with an error describing which parameters are out of range. Add particle effects inside the mug using a BufferGeometry Points system with animated Y positions to simulate bubbling. Enable OrbitControls with damping. Display a real-time parameter readout panel styled like a laboratory instrument display.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  temperature: number,         // current temperature in Celsius (0-500)
  stirringSpeed: number,         // rotation speed in RPM (0-100)
  filterTightness: number,         // compression level of molecular sieve (1-10)
  reagentConcentration: number,         // molarity of reagent solution (0.1, 0.5, 1.0, or 2.0)
  particleCount: number,         // number of reagent particles visible in vessel
  gearRotation: number,         // cumulative rotation angle of gears in radians
  filterScale: number,         // current X-axis scale of corset filter (0.7-1.3)
  extractionActive: boolean,         // whether extraction animation is currently running
  extractionProgress: number,         // progress of extraction animation (0-1)
  transferredParticles: number,         // count of particles that reached the filter during extraction
}
